content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

Verified both findings against the repo: `result` remains in the index on this branch, `git ls-files -i -c --exclude-standard` still prints `result`, and commit 7518cc15 merely updates the symlink target instead of running `git rm --cached`. Part B of the bead is unmet and is explicit acceptance criteria, so both amendments are accepted. They are duplicates of the same defect with the same correct remediation.

## Votes

- `fr-1-1cce7e4f`: Accept
  Confirmed by direct inspection: `git ls-files result` returns `result` and `git ls-files -i -c --exclude-standard` also returns `result`, so Part B of the bead (untracking `result`) was not implemented. The bead explicitly lists both parts as required and calls out 'result is no longer tracked on master (post-merge)' and 'git ls-files -i -c --exclude-standard on a fresh clone of master prints nothing' as acceptance criteria. The proposed remediation (`git rm --cached -- result` without deleting the working-tree symlink) exactly matches the bead's Part B instructions. High severity is appropriate — shipping only Part A reshapes rather than eliminates the checkpoint-diff noise the bead was created to remove.
- `fr-1-116d0ab2`: Accept
  Same defect as fr-1-1cce7e4f, independently verified: `result` is still in the index, the sole branch commit (7518cc15) changes the symlink target (`result | 2 +-`) instead of removing it from tracking, and the bead explicitly says 'both must land in this PR' with acceptance criteria that are currently unmet. The downstream-effect analysis is correct — future checkpoint trees built by the Part A logic will omit `result` against a parent that still tracks it, converting the symlink-target churn into a spurious `result` deletion in the diff. Accepting both duplicates is fine; the remediation will be applied once.
